name: Akkermansia muciniphila
key_rcts: Zhang Y et al. 2025 — Efficacy depends on its baseline levels in the gut (PMID 39879980, Cell Metab)
notes: Akkermansia muciniphila supplementation shows promise for managing type 2 diabetes and obesity, but more human clinical data are needed to fully understand its effects.

name: Alkalihalobacillus clausii
survivability_notes: Stability and delivery not a major concern, as probiotics are generally shelf-stable.
key_rcts: Maity C et al. 2021 — improved diarrheal conditions in children, adolescents and adults (PMID 34585011, Heliyon)
García G et al. 2024 — safety assessment for use as a probiotic in humans (PMID 39597688, Microorganisms)
Rathi A et al. 2025 — reduced symptoms of acute allergic rhinitis (PMID XXXXXXXX, Glob Adv Integr Med Health)
notes: Probiotic Alkalihalobacillus clausii 088AE shows therapeutic efficacy in antibiotic-associated diarrhea and alleviates symptoms of acute allergic rhinitis

name: Weizmannia coagulans
key_rcts: Xu Y et al. 2025 — Weizmannia coagulans BC01 improved functional constipation (PMID 41465910, Medicine)
notes: Weizmannia coagulans strain supported the management of functional constipation in ≥1 RCT with consistent positive results.

name: Bacillus indicus
survivability_notes: The probiotic supplement was able to survive and be effective in the human body.
key_rcts: Buhaș et al. 2023 — patients with psoriasis receiving probiotic and prebiotic supplementation performed better in measures of disease activity (PMID 37446403, Int J Mol Sci)

Campbell et al. 2020 — mild to moderately elevated triglyceride levels can be lowered in patients with mild to moderate hypertriglyceridemia by a probiotic supplement consisting of five different spore forming bacilli (PMID 33041703, Integr Med)
notes: Bacillus indicus strain shows promise for treating psoriasis and lowering triglyceride levels

name: Bacillus licheniformis
key_rcts: Buhaţ MC et al. 2023 — Psoriasis Area and Severity Index, Dermatology Life Quality Index improvements (PMID 37446403, Int J Mol Sci)
notes: Bacillus licheniformis has shown potential in treating psoriasis and maintaining gut microbiota balance

name: Priestia megaterium
key_rcts: Chen H et al. 2025 — robust uric acid degradation activity (PMID 40997969, Int J Biol Macromol)
notes: P. megaterium-based probiotics show potential for hyperuricemia management and tilapia growth

name: Bacillus subtilis
survivability_notes: No information on stability or delivery of the probiotic in this study.
key_rcts: Buhaś et al. 2023 — Patients with psoriasis receiving anti-psoriatic local therapy and probiotic and prebiotic supplementation performed better in measures of disease activity.

Garvey et al. 2022 — Supplementation with Bacillus subtilis BS50 increased the proportion of participants showing improvement from baseline to week 6 in the composite score for bloating, burping, and flatulence.
notes: Probiotics have shown promise in alleviating psoriasis symptoms and gastrointestinal symptoms. Bacillus subtilis has been studied as a probiotic strain in several human clinical trials.

name: Bifidobacterium adolescentis
key_rcts: Lee HJ et al. 2021 — probiotic NVP-1704 improved mental health and sleep in healthy adults (PMID 34444820, Nutrients)
notes: Probiotic NVP-1704 (mix of Lactobacillus reuteri NK33 and Bifidobacterium adolescentis NK98) shows benefits for depression, anxiety, insomnia, and cardiovascular disease. Additionally, it modulates the gut microbiota to improve sleep quality and reduce inflammation.

name: Bifidobacterium animalis subsp. lactis
key_rcts: Lai H et al. 2023 — mean increases of 0.95-1.05 in Bristol Stool Scale score (BSS) (PMID XXXXXXXX, Gut Microbes)
Vaz SR et al. 2024 — probiotics resulted in an average reduction of 51 min of crying per day (PMID XXXXXXXX, Acta Paediatr)
notes: Dietary fibers/probiotics may relieve constipation symptoms and infantile colic with intervention-specific changes in gut microbiota. Baseline gut microbiota may predispose the intervention responsiveness.

name: Bifidobacterium bifidum
key_rcts: Lukasik et al. 2022 — no effect on AAD (RR, 0.81; 95% CI, 0.49-1.33)
notes: Multispecies probiotic did not reduce the risk of AAD in children but reduced overall diarrhea risk during and after antibiotic treatment

name: Bifidobacterium breve
key_rcts: Tian et al. 2022 — B. breve CCFM1025 attenuates major depression disorder via regulating gut microbiome and tryptophan metabolism (PMID 34875345, Brain Behav Immun)
notes: Bifidobacterium breve CCFM1025 attenuates depression and associated gastrointestinal disorders, with mechanisms relevant to changes in the gut microbiome and tryptophan metabolism.

name: Bifidobacterium longum subsp. infantis
key_rcts: Kim CS et al. 2021 — improved cognitive function and mood (PMID: 32300799, J Gerontol A Biol Sci Med Sci)
Pinto-Sanchez MI et al. 2017 — reduced depression scores in patients with IBS (PMID: 28483500, Gastroenterology)
Kazemi A et al. 2019 — improved psychological outcomes in patients with major depressive disorder (PMID XXXXXXXX, Clin Nutr)
notes: Probiotics promote mental flexibility and alleviate stress, along with causing changes in gut microbiota, supporting health-promoting properties as a part of healthy diet for older adults.

name: Brevibacillus laterosporus
survivability_notes: Good tolerance to bile salt and simulated gastrointestinal fluid.
key_rcts: Liu Y et al. (2024) — B. laterosporus exhibits substantial potential as a probiotic activity in crop and animal production.
Cao Y et al. (2024) — The probiotic potential of B. laterosporus PBC01 was evaluated by characterizing hydrophobicity, auto-aggregation activity...
notes: The applications of B. laterosporus in animal production could be improved, necessitating further research to elucidate the underlying probiotic mechanisms.

name: Clostridium butyricum
effective_cfu_dose_label: high dose (≥ 5 billion CFUs per day)
key_rcts: Guo et al. 2019 — moderate protective effect of probiotics for preventing AAD (NNTB 9, 95% CI 7 to 13)
notes: Probiotics have been investigated for potential protective or ameliorative effects in a wide range of human diseases, with proposed mechanisms centered on molecular signals and immunological signals in the digestive system.

name: Enterococcus faecalis
key_rcts: Marashi et al. 2024 — alterations in the gut microbiome are associated with gastric cancer and precancerous lesions (PMID 39170038, Front Med)
Lu et al. 2025 — probiotic mixtures may be the most effective in reducing RQLQ, Total IgE, and Special IgE (PMID 39837158, Complement Ther Clin Pract)
notes: Probiotics have shown promising results in cancer prevention and treatment efficacy, but more human clinical data is needed for definitive conclusions

name: Enterococcus faecium
key_rcts: Deng X et al. 2024 — Prebiotics and synbiotics resulted in an increase in frequency of stool movements per week (PMID 38693449, Eur J Nutr)
notes: Probiotics, prebiotics, and synbiotics may help manage constipation; the effectiveness of Enterococcus faecium is not established by these studies

name: Lacticaseibacillus paracasei
key_rcts: Lukasik et al. 2022 — No effect on AAD risk in children; reduced overall diarrhea risk (PMID 35727573, JAMA Pediatr)
Yang et al. 2023 — Enhanced endothelial function and improved cardiometabolic health with L. paracasei supplementation (PMID 37506599, Clin Nutr)
Yang et al. 2023 — Potential benefits for Parkinson's disease management with L. paracasei Shirota supplementation (PMID XXXXXXXX, Food Funct)
notes: This strain has been shown to have beneficial effects on gut microbiome and clinical responses in various conditions, including antibiotic-associated diarrhea and cardiometabolic diseases. However, the evidence is not strong due to limited RCTs.

name: Lacticaseibacillus rhamnosus
key_rcts: Lukasik et al. 2022 — no effect on risk of AAD (RR 0.81; 95% CI 0.49-1.33), lower risk of diarrhea regardless of etiology
notes: Multispecies probiotic did not reduce the risk of AAD in children, but reduced overall risk of diarrhea.

name: Lactiplantibacillus plantarum
key_rcts: Martoni CJ et al. 2023 — Lactiplantibacillus plantarum (L. plantarum) improves IBS symptom severity with a dose-ranging effect (PMID 37576702, World J Gastroenterol)
Weber et al. 2023 — No direct evidence found in these abstracts
notes: A multispecies probiotic did not reduce the risk of AAD in children when analyzed according to the most stringent definition. However, it reduced the overall risk of diarrhea during and for 7 days after antibiotic treatment.

name: Lactobacillus acidophilus
key_rcts: Sniffen et al. 2018 — no effect on risk of AAD; Lactobacillus acidophilus W37 and W55 had a lower risk of diarrhea regardless of the etiology
notes: No significant effect of Lactobacillus acidophilus on antibiotic-associated diarrhea, but reduced risk of diarrhea in general

name: Lactobacillus delbrueckii subsp. bulgaricus
key_rcts: Gómez-Pérez AM et al. 2024 — no effect on insulin sensitivity (PMID 39458486, Nutrients)
notes: Limited evidence from a single clinical trial and a pilot study, suggesting potential benefits in pregnancy and upper respiratory diseases

name: Lactobacillus crispatus
key_rcts: Mändar et al. 2023 — Both oral and vaginal capsules reduced the signs and symptoms in BV patients.
Qi et al. 2023 — Oral administration of probiotics did not improve BV cure rates but restored vaginal health after cure.
Yang et al. 2020 — No information on efficacy or dose
notes: Limited human clinical data available for this strain, with most studies focusing on symptom relief rather than cure.

name: Lactobacillus gasseri
key_rcts: Qi et al. 2023 — Lactobacillus gasseri TM13 and Lactobacillus crispatus LG55 can restore the vaginal health of patients recovering from BV (PMID 37575226, Front Immunol)
notes: Two RCTs support the use of oral Lactobacillus strains for restoring vaginal health in patients with BV, but no evidence is presented on an effective CFU dose

name: Lactobacillus helveticus
key_rcts: Kazemi A et al. 2018 — probiotic supplementation resulted in a significant decrease in BDI score compared to placebo
Romijn AR et al. 2017 — no significant difference was found between the probiotic and placebo groups on any psychological outcome measure
notes: Only two abstracts discussed Lactobacillus helveticus, both with mixed findings; more studies are needed to determine its efficacy in treating Major Depressive Disorder

name: Lactobacillus jensenii
key_rcts: Laue et al. 2018 — Lactobacillus jensenii reduced the incidence of bacterial vaginosis (Fisher Exact test)
notes: Two clinical trials suggest that Lactobacillus jensenii may have beneficial effects on bacterial vaginosis, but high-quality evidence is needed to confirm these findings

name: Lactobacillus johnsonii
key_rcts: Guéniche A et al. 2009 - Lactobacillus johnsonii NCC 533 facilitates an earlier recovery of Epidermal cells allostimulatory function after UV exposure (PMID 20808516, Dermatoendocrinology)
notes: Lactobacillus johnsonii may contribute to modulate skin immune system, but evidence is limited. Probiotic supplementation might be useful in the management of atopic dermatitis.

name: Lactobacillus kefiranofaciens
key_rcts: Chen CW et al. 2025 — alleviates exercise and sleep deprivation-induced physiological dysregulation and neuropsychiatric disorders via modulation of inflammation, circadian rhythm, and stress response (PMID 40959574, Int J Med Sci)
notes: This strain has demonstrated probiotic traits and may serve as a nutritional strategy to mitigate health risks and enhance performance affected by sleep deprivation, particularly through regulation of inflammation, circadian rhythm, and stress response.

name: Lentilactobacillus parakefiri
key_rcts: Erdogan et al. 2019 — kefir produced from natural kefir grains showed higher TAS value and lower MPO values compared to starter culture
notes: This study found favorable effects of kefir produced from natural kefir grains on intestinal microbial populations and antioxidant capacities in Balb/c mice, but no clear evidence for the specific probiotic strain Lentilactobacillus parakefiri is presented

name: Lactococcus cremoris
key_rcts: Bousdouni et al. 2025 — potential anti-inflammatory effect (PMID 40572644, Medicina)
notes: Potential anti-inflammatory effect of Lactococcus cremoris spp. immobilized on oat flakes

name: Leuconostoc mesenteroides subsp. cremoris
key_rcts: Mo C et al. 2025 — sustained reduction in blood ethanol concentrations
Choi Y et al. 2025 — Increased lactate-producing bacteria
notes: Probiotic strain Leuconostoc mesenteroides VITA-PB2 shows efficacy in mitigating alcohol-induced oxidative stress and alleviating hangover-related symptoms

name: Leuconostoc mesenteroides subsp. dextranicum
survivability_notes: The study was conducted at room temperature and did not provide information on enteric coating. However, the authors suggest potential functional benefits as a substitute for traditional dairy products.
key_rcts: Mo et al. 2025 — VITA-PB2 significantly reduced acetaldehyde levels and improved hangover symptoms (PMID 40732900, Nutrients)
notes: Limited human clinical data; some methodological concerns in the included trials

name: Leuconostoc lactis
key_rcts: Jeong S et al. 2023 — synergistic immunostimulatory activities of probiotics (PMID 37317327, Microorganisms)
notes: Strain Leuconostoc lactis showed promising probiotic attributes, including resistance to acid and bile salts, broad utilization of prebiotics, and no hemolytic activity.

name: Leuconostoc mesenteroides
key_rcts: Mo et al. 2025 — sustained reduction in blood ethanol concentrations (PMID 40732900, Nutrients)
notes: Probiotic strain Leuconostoc mesenteroides VITA-PB2 shows promise in mitigating alcohol-induced oxidative stress and alleviating hangover-related symptoms, but further evidence is needed.

name: Levilactobacillus brevis
key_rcts: Honda et al. 2024 — Serum estrogen levels in healthy peri- and postmenopausal women; Casertano et al. 2024 — Effects of probiotics on depressive symptoms
notes: This strain has shown estrogen modulation capacity, psychotropic effects, and potential benefits for autism and ADHD. Further studies are required to confirm these findings.

name: Ligilactobacillus salivarius
key_rcts: Lukasik et al. 2022 — no effect on AAD, but reduced diarrhea risk (PMID 35727573, JAMA Pediatr)
notes: This strain shows promise for reducing diarrhea risk in children taking antibiotics, but its effectiveness for Atopic dermatitis is moderate due to limited RCTs.

name: Limosilactobacillus fermentum
survivability_notes: Longer-term studies including a higher number of infants are needed to determine whether the use of this probiotic during the early stages of life is an efficient way to reduce the incidence of GIs.
key_rcts: Pastor-Villaescusa B et al. 2021 — Limosilactobacillus fermentum CECT5716 reduces GI infections in infants (PMID 34208893, Microorganisms)
notes: Limosilactobacillus fermentum CECT5716 shows promise as a preventive therapy for GIs in infants with moderate evidence from this systematic review and meta-analysis.

name: Limosilactobacillus reuteri
key_rcts: Söderberg Veibäck et al. 2025 — no significant effects of probiotics relative to placebo (PMID 40484149, Brain Behav Immun)
notes: Findings are inconsistent and require further high-quality RCTs to confirm or refute the efficacy of Limosilactobacillus reuteri for inflammatory depression.

name: Pediococcus acidilactici
survivability_notes: No significant compositional changes were detected in fecal microbiota between probiotic and placebo.
key_rcts: Tian P et al. 2024 — Significant decrease in depression and anxiety scores, as well as ameliorated gastrointestinal dysfunction (PMID 39741947, Microbiome Res Rep)
notes: Pediococcus acidilactici has been shown to improve brain function by modulating gut microbiota and their mediated immune homeostasis in patients with depressive disorder

name: Pediococcus pentosaceus
key_rcts: Moreno-Villares JM(1) — The specific formulation of B. longum KABP042 and P. pentosaceus KABP041 achieved a higher response rate and a larger reduction in crying and fussing time in colicky infants.
Won SM(#)(1) — Next generation probiotics (Lactobacillus delbrueckii subsp. Lactis [LL001], L. helveticus [LH001], and Pediococcus pentosaceus KID7 [PPKID7]) on liver function parameters
notes: The evidence landscape for Pediococcus pentosaceus is limited to two abstracts, with both showing positive results in clinical trials.
More RCTs and meta-analyses are needed to fully understand the efficacy of Pediococcus pentosaceus in humans.

name: Propionibacterium freudenreichii subsp. shermanii
key_rcts: Kotowska-Bąbol M et al. 2025 — PROBAUT: a randomized controlled trial of multispecies probiotics in children with autism spectrum disorder (PMID 41422017, Trials)
notes: Probiotics may modulate gut microbiota and ameliorate autism symptoms; however, the evidence is limited to this single study

name: Saccharomyces cerevisiae var. boulardii
key_rcts: Xie P et al. 2023 — Lactobacillus acidophilus DDS-1 showed highest efficacy (SUCRA, 92.9%) in improving IBS Symptom Severity Scale
Alharbi BF et al. 2024 — Saccharomyces cerevisiae and S. boulardii showed protection rate of up to 39% against Traveler's Diarrhea
notes: Only a few probiotics that reduce TD risk or IBS-D symptoms exist, and the efficacy varies between strains

name: Kazachstania exigua
notes: This strain (Kazachstania exigua) is associated with sourdoughs and has metabolic properties contributing to flavour and potential nutritional and safety advantages, but no direct evidence on health benefits or effective dosage is presented in this abstract.

name: Kazachstania turicensis
survivability_notes: No information available on the stability or delivery of Saccharomyces turicensis CAU Y1706.
key_rcts: Kim JH et al. 2019 — ameliorates AD by regulation of gut-skin axis (PMID 30738679, Journal of Dairy Science)
notes: Kazachstania turicensis CAU Y1706 shows potential as a supplement for alleviation of atopic dermatitis

name: Kazachstania unispora
key_rcts: Wang F et al. 2025 — ameliorates patulin-induced intestinal injury (PMID 40179778, Journal of Hazardous Materials)
notes: A novel dietary Se carrier, Se-enriched Kazachstania unispora KU2, exhibits probiotic potential and alleviates PAT-induced inflammatory infiltration, gut microbiota disruption, and metabolic imbalances in mice

name: Enterococcus faecalis

key_rcts.Author: Marashi et al. 2024 — gut microbiome alterations and treatment outcomes (PMID 39170038, Front Med Lausanne)
key_rcts.Key Finding: Altered gut microbiome associated with gastric cancer; probiotics improve treatment efficacy

key_rcts.Author: Lu et al. 2025 — Probiotic mixtures for allergic rhinitis (PMID 39837158, Complement Ther Clin Pract)
key_rcts.Key Finding: Probiotic mixtures reduce TNSS, RQLQ, and Special IgE; Saccharomyces and Lactobacillus also show efficacy

key_rcts.Author: Dowgiałło-Gornowicz et al. 2024 — Probiotics after sleeve gastrectomy (PMID XXXXXXXX, Nutrients)

notes: Enterococcus faecalis shows promise as a probiotic strain for various health conditions, including gastric cancer and allergic rhinitis.

name: Streptococcus salivarius
key_rcts: Babina K et al. 2023 — Oral probiotic with Streptococcus salivarius M18 reduces gingival inflammation (PMID XXXXXXXX, Nutrients)
notes: Oral probiotic containing Streptococcus salivarius M18 strain may have antigingivitis and antiplaque effects.

name: Streptococcus thermophilus
key_rcts: Alemu BK et al. 2023 — The overall effect of probiotics on the beneficial bacteria detection rate in breast milk had a risk difference of 24% (95% confidence interval, 0.1-0.37; P<.001)
Skrzydło-Radomańska B et al. 2021 — The multi-strain probiotic intervention resulted in a significant improvement in IBS symptoms evaluated with the use of both IBS-SSS and IBS-GIS scales
notes: Maternal probiotic supplements effectively orchestrate the breast milk and infant gut microbiome with a wide range of clinical benefits.

name: Lactobacillus delbrueckii subsp. lactis
key_rcts: Gómez-Pérez AM et al. 2024 — FMT from healthy lean subjects in patients with Type 2 Diabetes (PMID 39458486, Nutrients)
Ma G et al. 2024 — Probiotic supplementation during pregnancy and gut microbiota functional diversity (PMID 38687069, Microbiol Spectr)
notes: Probiotics for Type 2 Diabetes: Mixed findings across RCTs. Probiotics during pregnancy: Significant impact on functional metabolism, but no human clinical data for insulin sensitivity.

name: Lactococcus lactis
key_rcts: Allam AR et al. 2025 — greatest reduction in fasting plasma glucose with Lactococcus lactis W19 and W58 combinations (PMID 40646644, Diabetol Metab Syndr)
notes: Probiotics, including Lactococcus lactis strains, may improve glycemic control and lipid profiles in patients with Type 2 diabetes mellitus. However, findings should be interpreted with caution due to variability in study designs and potential risk of bias.
